intro: I am an inquisitive engineer who likes building systems, writing code that stays understandable, and making technical work legible instead of invisible.
summary: Backend work is usually buried behind interfaces. This portfolio treats it like an atlas: routes, constraints, tools, and tradeoffs made visible without losing readability.
principles: Reliability over novelty when systems matter.
Architecture should be explainable, not mystical.
Product context matters as much as technical depth.
The most interesting engineering work usually lives in tradeoffs.
notes: I enjoy software development because it gives me room to explore ideas and build useful things, from practical backend systems to more experimental projects.
The final copy here can stay concise on first read, then expand into deeper notes when the user asks for more context.
This section is intentionally configured from JSON so the tone and emphasis can evolve without touching layout code.
